questions.question: Which 1789 event in Paris is widely regarded as the symbolic start of the French Revolution?
questions.explanation: The Storming of the Bastille on July 14, 1789, became a powerful symbol of revolt against royal authority.
questions.hostLeadIn: Time to kick off a revolution.
questions.explanationStyled: The Storming of the Bastille. Nothing says 'we're done here' like taking over a prison and making it symbolic forever.

questions.question: Which period of relative peace and stability began in the Roman Empire under Augustus and lasted about 200 years?
choices: The Crisis of the Third Century
questions.explanation: The Pax Romana was a long period of stability and minimal expansion under Roman rule.
questions.explanationStyled: The Pax Romana. About two centuries where Rome decided to chill… by its standards, anyway.

questions.question: From which country did the United States acquire the Louisiana Territory in 1803?
questions.explanation: The United States purchased the Louisiana Territory from France in 1803.
questions.hostLeadIn: Biggest real estate deal in U.S. history.
questions.explanationStyled: France sold it. Napoleon needed money, Jefferson doubled the country. Pretty solid transaction.

questions.question: Which Babylonian king is associated with one of the earliest written legal codes?
questions.explanation: Hammurabi created one of the earliest known legal codes around 1754 BCE.
questions.explanationStyled: Hammurabi. Eye for an eye, and suddenly everyone’s paying attention to the rules.

questions.question: Which 1884–1885 meeting regulated European colonization in Africa?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Conference established rules for European colonization in Africa.
questions.explanationStyled: The Berlin Conference. A room full of Europeans dividing a continent like it was a group project.

questions.question: Which element has the chemical symbol 'Au'?
questions.explanation: 'Au' comes from the Latin word 'aurum,' meaning gold.
questions.explanationStyled: Gold. From 'aurum,' because scientists love Latin almost as much as they love gold.

questions.question: Which nitrogenous base pairs with cytosine in DNA?
questions.explanation: Cytosine pairs with guanine via three hydrogen bonds.
questions.explanationStyled: Guanine. Three hydrogen bonds. Strong, stable, and way more reliable than most relationships.

questions.question: Which law of thermodynamics states that entropy approaches zero at absolute zero?
questions.explanation: The third law states entropy approaches zero for a perfect crystal at absolute zero.
questions.explanationStyled: The Third Law. Absolute zero, zero chaos. The universe briefly gets its act together.

questions.question: Which layer of Earth's atmosphere contains the ozone layer?
questions.explanation: The ozone layer is located in the stratosphere.
questions.explanationStyled: The stratosphere. Ozone up there quietly blocking radiation like a full-time bodyguard.

questions.question: Which mineral has a hardness of 9 on the Mohs scale?
questions.explanation: Corundum is rated 9, just below diamond at 10.
questions.explanationStyled: Corundum. Diamond gets the spotlight at 10, but this one’s right behind it doing the real work.

questions.question: In Toy Story, what is the name of the space ranger toy who thinks he is real?
questions.explanation: Buzz Lightyear initially believes he is a real space ranger.
questions.hostLeadIn: To infinity… and basic identity confusion.
questions.explanationStyled: Buzz Lightyear. Absolute confidence, zero self-awareness. We’ve all met that guy.

questions.question: Which artist released the album "21" in 2011?
questions.explanation: Adele’s album "21" became one of the best-selling albums of all time.
questions.explanationStyled: Adele. She turned heartbreak into a global event and a ridiculous number of sales.

questions.question: Who starred alongside Willem Dafoe in the 2019 film "The Lighthouse"?
questions.explanation: Robert Pattinson co-starred in the psychological horror film.
questions.hostLeadIn: Things get weird on this island.
questions.explanationStyled: Robert Pattinson. From vampire heartthrob to full-on lighthouse madness. Quite the pivot.

questions.question: Which NBA team has won the most championships as of 2024?
questions.explanation: The Boston Celtics hold the record with 18 championships.
questions.explanationStyled: Boston Celtics. Eighteen titles and absolutely no intention of being quiet about it.

questions.question: Who was the first tennis player to achieve a Golden Slam in a single year?
questions.explanation: Steffi Graf achieved the Golden Slam in 1988.
questions.explanationStyled: Steffi Graf. All four majors plus Olympic gold. That’s not dominance, that’s overkill.

questions.question: Which country hosted and won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930?
questions.explanation: Uruguay both hosted and won the inaugural World Cup.
questions.hostLeadIn: Back to where it all began.
questions.explanationStyled: Uruguay. Hosted it, won it, set the tone. Home-field advantage taken very seriously.

questions.question: Which DNS record type maps a domain name to an IPv6 address?
questions.explanation: AAAA records map domain names to IPv6 addresses.
questions.explanationStyled: AAAA. Because IPv6 needed more space, and apparently more A’s.

questions.question: What does PGP stand for in email encryption?
questions.explanation: PGP stands for Pretty Good Privacy, a widely used encryption system.
questions.explanationStyled: Pretty Good Privacy. Undersold name for something that fundamentally changed secure communication.
